import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from scipy.stats import norm
from itertools import chain

def task_func(L):
    # Flatten the list of lists
    flattened_list = list(chain.from_iterable(L))

    # Fit a normal distribution to the data
    data = np.array(flattened_list)
    fitted_mean, fitted_std = norm.fit(data)
    y = norm.pdf(np.arange(min(data), max(data), 0.1), fitted_mean, fitted_std)

    # Create a figure and axes
    fig, ax = plt.subplots()

    # Plot histogram of the data with the fitted normal distribution overlay
    ax.hist(flattened_list, bins=50, density=True, color='blue')
    ax.plot(np.arange(min(data), max(data), 0.1), y, 'r--', linewidth=2)

    # Set the title and labels for the plot
    ax.set_title('Histogram with Fitted Normal Distribution')
    ax.set_xlabel('Data')
    ax.set_ylabel('Probability Density')

    return ax, flattened_list
